page: Amazon Web Services r6g.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton2 processors utilizing the Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture running at 2.5 GHz. It features 32 dedicated physical cores with 32 vCPUs, meaning hyperthreading is disabled with a single thread per core. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local NVMe storage, has no GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates solid multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 48584.84 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11647.0, both placing in the average tier. Memory bandwidth is notable, with cached small-block reads reaching 1893.45 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 165.0 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 950.0 and a weak rating in single-threaded gzip compression. Database operations are robust, with Passmark database operations scoring 20399.74 and Redis SET operations processing 37.16 million operations per second.

Built on the ARM64 architecture, the r6g.8xlarge offers a cost-efficient alternative to traditional x86 memory-optimized instances, delivering high memory density and dedicated core allocation without the overhead of hyperthreading. Due to its high memory capacity and strong multi-threaded performance, this instance is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads such as open-source databases, in-memory caches like Redis, real-time big data analytics, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r6g.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores on the 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton2 architecture running at 2.5 GHz. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 256.0 GB of RAM, representing an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio, with a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core and memory capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48584.84 and a Passmark memory score of 1824.3, though single-core performance remains in the lower tier. The instance demonstrates strong database capabilities, processing 37.16 million Redis SET operations per second. This profile makes the r6g.8xlarge a highly cost-efficient choice for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, and distributed data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.8xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 instance with 32 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for in-memory databases and caching.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.8xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated cores, 256 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance delivering dedicated physical cores and high memory density for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton2 ARMv8 physical cores at 2.5 GHz
Equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM for memory-intensive applications
Delivers 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong multi-threaded performance with average database and Redis throughput
Optimized for in-memory caches, databases, and real-time data processing
